description: Added two bullet sub-points under item 5 (Thoughts/Feelings/Intentions/Suspicions) and two bullet sub-points under item 6 (Plans and Decisions) with concrete examples of incorrect merging behavior and explicit instructions to separate thought/decision entries from action entries.

Expanded the 'If the transcript is uncertain' bullet in the Live Session Log section into a full 'Preserve uncertainty explicitly' sub-section with four concrete examples: ambiguous success (use 'appeared to'), unclear speaker identity (use description without asserting identity), perception vs reality ('believed' vs stated fact), and partial outcomes ('may have escaped' vs 'escaped').

Added one bullet to the Critical Requirements section: 'Do not over-resolve uncertainty: when the transcript is unclear about a speaker's identity, whether an outcome succeeded, or whether a perception reflects reality, write the entry with the same level of certainty (or uncertainty) the transcript carries — use hedged wording like appeared to, seemed to, believed, or reportedly rather than asserting a definite fact.'

Added one bullet to the Recaps section: 'Do not collapse multiple recap facts into a single entry. Each separate event, discovery, decision, clue, relationship, and prior development should be its own entry. If a character found a clue AND decided to pursue a lead, those are two entries, not one.'

Rewrote the Repeated Non-Information rule in What to Remove from 'Collapse pure repetition that adds no new fictional information' to 'Collapse only exact or near-exact repetition — where the same in-game fact is restated word-for-word or in trivially equivalent phrasing with no new information added. Do not collapse two related but distinct beats just because they concern the same topic.'

Added one bullet to the Live Session Log section: 'Do not skip small, incremental, or transitional in-game events. A character moving to a location, a door being found locked, an NPC brief in-character statement, or a minor environmental detail are all required entries if the transcript states them. Include them even if they seem unimportant.'

Added a sub-bullet under item 3 (Perceptions and Observations) in What to Capture: 'Separate observation entries from action entries: Do not merge an observation with the action that follows it. Kira noticed the door was ajar, then slipped through should produce two entries: one for noticing the door, one for slipping through.'

Added one bullet to the Live Session Log section specifically about Indeterminate_Speaker attribution: 'When the transcript labels a speaker as Indeterminate_Speaker or otherwise fails to identify who is speaking, do not guess or infer the speaker's identity — write the entry using a description like an unidentified voice or someone in the group rather than assigning the statement to a specific character. Only attribute a statement to a named character if the transcript itself identifies them as the speaker.'

Added one sentence to the schema preamble: 'Each entry in both arrays must be a single complete sentence. Each distinct in-game beat — including individual actions, events, observations, speeches, stated thoughts, stated decisions, and consequences — must be its own entry.'

Added one sub-bullet under item 1 (Actions) in What to Capture: 'Do not chain multiple actions into one entry: If a character moved to a location AND then picked up an object, those are two entries, not one. Write one entry per distinct action.'

Added a sentence to item 3 (Perceptions and Observations) in What to Capture: 'Write perceptions as perceptions, not as confirmed facts: if a character sees or hears something, that character perceived it — do not upgrade a perception into a confirmed world-fact unless the transcript confirms it independently.'
